title: Living in Boulder blog 7-21-2017: 1st ride on new bike. Up Boulder Canyon , down 72.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his first ride on a new road bike, covering 54.7 miles up Boulder Canyon and down Highway 72 in about 4 hours 11 minutes. He discusses issues with his watt pedals not fitting the new bike, website organization of his rides, and his plan to create Strava segments for full Boulder-area routes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Nobody makes Strava segments for complete Boulder-area loops; Kyle sees himself as the first to do so and thinks it's a good idea
Frustrated that total ride time isn't shown on the main screen and requires extra clicks
Annoyed that his new mainstream bike (Merits/Marin 69) doesn't work with his watt pedals, which was the main reason he got it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went on my first ride with my new road bike today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The ride was 54.7 miles with 43.54 feet of elevation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The ride took 4 hours, 11 minutes, and 25 seconds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got up late because I couldn't breathe less than I think it happened again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did bike stuff, they called me and I went down and got the adapter to be able to put the bike on the rollers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I noticed that the pedal was hitting the change, just like with my other bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The reason I'm riding again is because I wanted to get a lot pedals for my other bike to make riding on the rollers interesting, but they didn't fit them either.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took them to the bike shop and they said they've never seen anything like that before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to take the little sensor thing off of the leather right pedal and call them tomorrow to see if I can get it to work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I also got another bag and some new gloves.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't gotten my helmet in the mail yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to get organized and stuff on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I noticed that on Google Earth, I needed to re-color all the road rides, but then realized I didn't have to do that because it was just the main file below.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was still getting oriented with the whole oversight of the whole website again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can make a new page of rides that I do, but I only had made rides of very big rides before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've turned my ride into a Strava segment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When people make Strava segments, they don't make them for the whole ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm the first person to make Strava segments for whole rides like from Boulder up to the net or over the wall and back.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My computer is running really loud and I wonder if the microphone will pick it up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow, I'll set up the rollers on the bike, the watt pedals, and the heart rate monitor.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to readjust when I do rollers because I try to fix it with access to screw you up some.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll go on a ride, do a Strava segment, come up with a route, and do the video before I do it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll use the second segment to go to and then talk about it after, which is my new thing that I'll do.
